pattern: A subsequence OR can only ever turn a bit on if some chosen element already has that exact bit pattern contained within it, so a lone power of two is only ever reachable when that exact power of two appears as an element.
hints: The OR of any group of numbers can only set a bit if at least one number in the group has that bit set; think about what it would take for the OR of a group to equal exactly a single power of two, with every other bit off.
For the OR of a chosen group to equal exactly 2^k, every chosen number's bits must fit inside just that one bit position — meaning every chosen number would have to equal 2^k itself.
So a power of two is expressible if and only if it appears literally in the array. Check 1, 2, 4, 8, ... in order against the set of given values and return the first one missing.

edge_inputs.description: single element that is not a power of two
value has the low bit set but is not itself a power of two
every power of two except the smallest is present
large non-power values cannot substitute for a missing small power

stress.description: uniformly random values across the full range
shuffled small powers of two plus random filler, exercising the scan far up the chain
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    powers = [1 << k for k in range(0, 20)]
    rng.shuffle(powers)
    take = min(n, len(powers))
    chosen = powers[:take]
    extra = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n - take)]
    return [chosen + extra]
